_meta.note: Curated architecture table merged with live fs.stat. Regenerate after adding a dataset or tool.

nodes.label: CMS Care Compare
nodes.plain: The federal government's public list of every Medicare-certified hospital. We download it, clean it, and merge it into our one hospital file.
nodes.detail: Hospital General Information download. One leg of the us-hospitals merge.

nodes.label: CMS ASC Dataset
nodes.plain: CMS's public list of Medicare-certified outpatient surgery centers. The raw material behind us-ascs.json.
nodes.detail: Ambulatory surgical center listing behind us-ascs.json.

nodes.label: CMS Dialysis Dataset
nodes.plain: CMS's public list of dialysis clinics, including which chain runs each one. Becomes us-dialysis.json.
nodes.detail: Dialysis facility compare data behind us-dialysis.json.

nodes.label: CMS DMEPOS CSV
nodes.plain: CMS's directory of medical equipment suppliers, pharmacies, and optical shops. A build script downloads the current copy and splits it into four map layers.
nodes.detail: Supplier directory CSV; build-suppliers.js resolves the current download URL from the CMS metastore (the raw URL carries a rotating hash).

nodes.label: CDC PLACES
nodes.plain: CDC's county-by-county health estimates: diabetes, obesity, checkups, and more. A pull script refreshes our county metrics from it.
nodes.detail: County health measures via the Socrata API (swc5-untb).

nodes.label: BLS LAUS
nodes.plain: The Bureau of Labor Statistics' county unemployment numbers. One feeder of the county metrics file.
nodes.detail: Local Area Unemployment Statistics, county grain.

nodes.label: Census SAIPE
nodes.plain: Census income and poverty estimates for every county. Another feeder of the county metrics file.
nodes.detail: Small Area Income and Poverty Estimates.

nodes.label: Census SAHIE
nodes.plain: Census health-insurance coverage estimates for every county. Another feeder of the county metrics file.
nodes.detail: Small Area Health Insurance Estimates.

nodes.label: Census FIPS / us-atlas
nodes.plain: The government's numbering system for states and counties, plus the map shapes. The spine every geographic file on this site joins on.
nodes.detail: County FIPS spine + us-atlas TopoJSON, pulled from the CDN by build-geo.js.

nodes.label: AHRQ Compendium 2023
nodes.plain: A federal research file that says which health system owns which hospital. Merged into the hospital file so the map can browse by system.
nodes.detail: Health-system crosswalk merged into us-hospitals.json.

nodes.label: HIFLD / CovidCareMap
nodes.plain: Federal infrastructure data carrying bed counts and facility locations. Another leg of the hospital merge.
nodes.detail: Bed counts and facility geometry merged into us-hospitals.json.

nodes.label: BLS OOH
nodes.plain: The government's career handbook: typical pay and projected job growth for every occupation. Powers the Career Tree's salary and outlook layers.
nodes.detail: Occupational Outlook Handbook pay + growth behind the career tree BLS layers.

nodes.label: Hand-Curated (editorial)
nodes.plain: Everything written by hand for this site: the career tree structure, the vendor list, the metric ledger, the atlas concepts, the citation ledger. The editorial layer.
nodes.detail: The editorial layer: vendors, career tree structure, atlas concepts, registries, metric ledger.

nodes.label: pull/places.js
nodes.plain: Fetches fresh CDC county health numbers and writes them into countyData.json. Run when CDC publishes a new year.
nodes.detail: Refreshable CDC PLACES pull. Dry-run by default; writes county metrics into countyData.json.

nodes.label: pull/bls.js
nodes.plain: Fetches county unemployment from BLS and writes it into countyData.json.
nodes.detail: Refreshable BLS LAUS pull. Dry-run by default.

nodes.label: pull/saipe.js
nodes.plain: Fetches county income estimates from the Census into countyData.json.
nodes.detail: Refreshable Census SAIPE pull. Dry-run by default.

nodes.label: pull/sahie.js
nodes.plain: Fetches county uninsured rates from the Census into countyData.json.
nodes.detail: Refreshable Census SAHIE pull. Dry-run by default.

nodes.label: build-suppliers.js
nodes.plain: Downloads the CMS supplier directory, collapses duplicate storefronts into single points, and splits the result into the four supplier map layers.
nodes.detail: Fetch-on-build; resolves the CMS DMEPOS URL via the dataset metastore (pinned URL is fallback only), then splits the directory into four supplier datasets.

nodes.label: build-geo.js
nodes.plain: Downloads county map shapes and population, and writes the geographic base files the maps draw.
nodes.detail: Pulls us-atlas TopoJSON from the CDN; writes the county population and lakes basemap files.

nodes.label: build-data.js
nodes.plain: The adder-upper: facilities per state, per county, per system. Its outputs feed page templates while the site builds.
nodes.detail: Rollups: facilities by state / county / system, national summary, suppliers by state. Build-only outputs.

nodes.label: build-entities.js
nodes.plain: The big joiner. Reads every major dataset, links hospitals to states to metrics to careers into one graph, and writes the Atlas search file. Refuses to build if any link dangles.
nodes.detail: 10-adapter unified graph. Reads every major dataset, validates links (incl. the cross-links metric-name guard), writes the search graph.

nodes.label: us-hospitals.json
nodes.plain: Every U.S. hospital in one file: name, location, type, beds, star rating, owning system. The Hospital Operations Map loads it the moment the page opens.
nodes.detail: 5,366 hospitals. CMS Care Compare merged with the AHRQ system crosswalk and HIFLD/CovidCareMap beds.

nodes.label: us-ascs.json
nodes.plain: Outpatient surgery centers. Sits on the shelf until you toggle that layer on the map.
nodes.detail: 5,611 ambulatory surgical centers.

nodes.label: us-dialysis.json
nodes.plain: Dialysis clinics with their chains. Loads on the map when toggled.
nodes.detail: 7,557 dialysis facilities.

nodes.label: us-suppliers-pharmacy.json
nodes.plain: Every Medicare-enrolled pharmacy, 40,000+ points. The heaviest file on the site, which is exactly why it only loads when you ask for it, one state at a time.
nodes.detail: 40,606 pharmacy locations. The single heaviest deployed file on the site.

nodes.label: us-suppliers-dme.json
nodes.plain: Home medical equipment suppliers. Loads on toggle.
nodes.detail: 6,367 DME supplier locations.

nodes.label: us-suppliers-optical.json
nodes.plain: Optical and vision suppliers. Loads on toggle.
nodes.detail: 5,023 optical supplier locations.

nodes.label: us-suppliers-orthotics-prosthetics.json
nodes.plain: Orthotics and prosthetics suppliers. Loads on toggle.
nodes.detail: 4,566 orthotics and prosthetics locations.

nodes.label: us-counties.json
nodes.plain: The population of every county. The maps use it for labels and per-person math.
nodes.detail: County population spine.

nodes.label: us-lakes.json
nodes.plain: Lakes and coastline shapes, so the maps read as maps.
nodes.detail: Basemap water geometry.

nodes.label: countyData.json
nodes.plain: County health, income, and coverage metrics across three lenses. Both maps tint counties with it, and the four pull scripts are what keep it fresh.
nodes.detail: County metrics across 3 lenses. The one dataset the four pull scripts refresh.

nodes.label: career-tree.json
nodes.plain: The Career Tree itself: 158 real roles, their families and tiers, and the promotion roads between them. Edit this file and the tool redraws.
nodes.detail: The tiered-hex career structure: roles, tiers, paths.

nodes.label: career-tree-bls.json
nodes.plain: Real pay and job growth per role, matched to government occupation codes. The Career Tree's salary heatmap.
nodes.detail: BLS pay + outlook enrichment keyed role → SOC.

nodes.label: career-tree-growth-detail.json
nodes.plain: The how-to text behind each expertise tile: how you build the skill, how you show it.
nodes.detail: Per-tile growth how/show enrichment.

nodes.label: career-tree-creds.json
nodes.plain: The credential reality file: exam pass rates, fees, program lengths, and what getting into each program takes. Powers the credential panels, the plan's bill, and the Getting-in checklists.
nodes.detail: Credential reality: pass rates, fees, program lengths, entry requirements. Sourced from the boards themselves; feeds the panels, the bill, and the Getting-in checklists.

nodes.label: vendors.json
nodes.plain: The healthcare-tech vendor directory: 117 companies across 20 sectors, with sourced market-share notes.
nodes.detail: 117 vendors across 20 healthcare-tech sectors.

nodes.label: derived/search-graph.json
nodes.plain: One big web connecting everything on this page: hospitals, states, metrics, careers, concepts. Atlas search reads it, and it is only fetched when someone actually searches.
nodes.detail: 23,090-node unified graph. The one build-entities artifact that deploys; lazy-loaded since 2026-07.

nodes.label: stateData.json
nodes.plain: State numbers for all seven lenses of the Multi-Lens Map. Baked directly into that page when the site builds, so the map opens with zero data fetches.
nodes.detail: 7 lenses of state metrics, sourced per the metricsConfig ledger. EMBEDDED into the Multi-Lens Map at build.

nodes.label: metricsConfig.json
nodes.plain: The ledger for those numbers: what each metric is, which source it came from, and when we retrieved it. The provenance system.
nodes.detail: The provenance ledger: every metric, its source, year, and units.

nodes.label: dataYears.json
nodes.plain: Which year each metric's data actually comes from. Keeps the maps honest about vintage.
nodes.detail: Data-vintage year stamps embedded at build.

nodes.label: references.js
nodes.plain: Every outside source the site cites, in one list with per-article tags. The /learn/sources/ page is rendered from it at build.
nodes.detail: The citation ledger: every external source the site cites, half extracted from metricsConfig, half curated with per-article consumers. Renders the /learn/sources/ database at build.

nodes.label: registries/ (6 files)
nodes.plain: The shared spelling of things: state codes, county IDs, health-system names, facility types. Every other file agrees with these six.
nodes.detail: The shared spine: geo, counties, systems, facility-types, ownership, taxonomy. The backbone adapter reads these.

nodes.label: entities.json
nodes.plain: The full typed graph before trimming. Too big to ship to browsers; instead it is injected into Atlas Craft while the site builds.
nodes.detail: The typed node graph. Lives outside src/ on purpose: NOT deployed. Injected into Atlas Craft at build.

nodes.label: search-index.json
nodes.plain: A build-time search helper. Never deployed; the live site uses the smaller search graph instead.
nodes.detail: Build-time search index. NOT deployed; the live atlas fetches search-graph.json instead.

nodes.label: facilities-by-state.json
nodes.plain: Facilities added up per state. Used while building pages, never shipped to browsers.
nodes.detail: Build-only rollup consumed by src/_data at template time. NOT deployed.

nodes.label: facilities-by-county.json
nodes.plain: Facilities added up per county. Build-time only.
nodes.detail: Build-only rollup. NOT deployed.

nodes.label: facilities-by-system.json
nodes.plain: Facilities added up per health system. Build-time only.
nodes.detail: Build-only rollup. NOT deployed.

nodes.label: national-summary.json
nodes.plain: The national totals a few pages quote. Computed at build, never shipped.
nodes.detail: Build-only national rollup. NOT deployed.

nodes.label: suppliers-by-state.json
nodes.plain: Supplier counts per state: the small summary the graph builder reads so it never has to open the four big supplier files.
nodes.detail: Build-only supplier rollup; the suppliers adapter reads this, not the four big files.

nodes.label: Operators Map
nodes.plain: The facility map. Opens with hospitals loaded; every other layer streams in only when you toggle it.
nodes.detail: The facility map. Eager basemap + hospitals; every other layer lazy-loads on toggle.

nodes.label: Multi-Lens Map
nodes.plain: The state choropleth with seven lenses. State data rides inside the page itself; county detail arrives at startup.
nodes.detail: State choropleth, 7 lenses. State data rides in the HTML at build; county grain fetches at init.

nodes.label: Career Tree
nodes.plain: The career planner. Loads all four career files up front, then everything runs in the browser.
nodes.detail: Tiered-hex role progression with BLS pay/outlook enrichment.

nodes.label: Vendor Directory
nodes.plain: The vendor browser: one data file, loaded on open.
nodes.detail: 20 sectors, 117 vendors, sourced market-share carousel.

nodes.label: Atlas (search + HUD)
nodes.plain: The concept atlas. Browsing costs nothing; search taps the big graph, fetched lazily on first use.
nodes.detail: The hex atlas. Search rides the unified graph, lazy-fetched on first use.

nodes.label: Atlas Craft
nodes.plain: The relationship-explorer canvas. Its entire dataset was baked into the page at build time.
nodes.detail: Relationship-explorer canvas. The entity graph is injected into the template at build.

nodes.label: Hospital Map
nodes.plain: The older camera-zoom hospital map. Self-contained.
nodes.detail: Camera-zoom map. No external data files.

nodes.label: Clinical SQL Mystery
nodes.plain: The SQL teaching game. All data is synthetic, on purpose. Nothing fetched.
nodes.detail: Synthetic in-page data. Nothing fetched.

nodes.label: Iceberg Map
nodes.plain: The hand-drawn concept map. No data files at all.
nodes.detail: Hand-drawn concept map. No data files.

nodes.label: Learn / Rounds pages
nodes.plain: The article pages. Any live statistic they quote was pulled from the state data while the site built.
nodes.detail: Editorial pages that pull stateData-derived stats at build.
